source_jobs.title: Senior AI Enablement Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us

ClearBank was built on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it’s the catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. That’s why our clients — financial institutions from fintechs and crypto platforms, to banks and credit unions — use our API to power their banking infrastructure.

But we wouldn’t be ClearBank without our people. They’re what powers our innovative technology and the reason we love what we do every day. We’re a group of spirited people who are never afraid to challenge the norm – becoming stronger, more energised, and that much better when we’re together. It’s our belief in fairness, autonomy and choice that means our people are empowered with the tools to learn, grow, and contribute to ours and our clients’ success. Interested in joining us? Read on or visit our website for more information.


About you

You’ll be joining the Developer Experience team as an AI Enablement Engineer (Level 4). Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you’ll be part of a fast-growing business that is challenging the market and doing things differently.

You are a senior engineer motivated by improving how software is built at scale, not just writing code yourself. You are deeply curious about AI, pragmatic about its application, and focused on driving measurable outcomes rather than novelty.

You enjoy partnering closely with engineers and leaders, understanding delivery workflows end to end, and shaping how teams adopt new tools and techniques safely and effectively. You are comfortable operating in a regulated environment and balancing enablement with appropriate governance.

You are at your best when acting as a multiplier — setting direction, coaching others, influencing practices across teams, and remaining hands-on when needed to remove blockers or prove out new approaches.

You will be

· Lead the effective and responsible use of AI across ClearBank’s software engineering teams.

· Act as a subject-matter expert on AI-assisted software engineering practices, tooling, and adoption patterns.

· Shape how teams embed AI into the SDLC in ways that improve productivity, quality, and developer experience.

· Drive alignment with stakeholders so AI adoption delivers measurable outcomes rather than anecdotal gains.

· Champion pragmatic governance that enables progress while meeting regulatory and risk expectations.

· Influence engineering practices across teams and help build a coherent AI enablement approach across the bank.


What you will be doing

· Master and evaluate AI tooling used in software engineering, including copilots, agentic tools, and workflow-integrated capabilities.

· Collaborate directly with engineering teams to improve how AI is used in coding, testing, review, debugging, and documentation.

· Introduce new AI tools, techniques, or approaches into the engineering community and support adoption at scale.

· Define and measure success using indicators such as DORA and flow metrics, adoption and engagement signals, and code quality or operational outcomes.

· Partner closely with Security, Model Risk, and other stakeholders to keep controls proportionate and non-blocking.

· Occasionally build or extend missing capabilities, including AI-driven services, agents, or platform enhancements that integrate into the SDLC.

· Build relationships with other teams across the bank using AI to share approaches, avoid duplication, and support coherent investment decisions.

· Coach and guide engineers on effective patterns, helping raise capability across the engineering organisation.


What we are looking for

· Strong background in software engineering, platform engineering, DevEx, or DevOps.

· Hands-on experience using AI-assisted development tools in real engineering environments.

· Experience influencing practices and improving outcomes across multiple delivery teams.

· Ability to evaluate tools and approaches based on evidence and business impact, not hype.

·

source_jobs.title: AI Enablement Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us

ClearBank was built on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it’s the catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. That’s why our clients — financial institutions from fintechs and crypto platforms, to banks and credit unions — use our API to power their banking infrastructure.

But we wouldn’t be ClearBank without our people. They’re what powers our innovative technology and the reason we love what we do every day. We’re a group of spirited people who are never afraid to challenge the norm – becoming stronger, more energised, and that much better when we’re together. It’s our belief in fairness, autonomy and choice that means our people are empowered with the tools to learn, grow, and contribute to ours and our clients’ success. Interested in joining us? Read on or visit our website for more information.


About you

You’ll be joining the Developer Experience team as an AI Enablement Engineer. Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you’ll be part of a fast-growing business that is challenging the market and doing things differently.

You are a strong mid-level engineer who enjoys improving how software is built, not just delivering code in isolation. You are curious about AI, practical in how you apply it, and focused on helping teams use it safely and effectively in real engineering workflows.

You enjoy collaborating with other engineers, understanding how they work, and sharing approaches that improve productivity, quality, and developer experience. You are happy being hands-on, while growing your ability to influence engineering practices more broadly.

You are comfortable working in a regulated environment and understand that enablement must be balanced with appropriate governance and controls.


You will be

· Support the effective and responsible use of AI across ClearBank’s software engineering teams.

· Work day-to-day with engineers to guide how AI can be applied within real delivery workflows.

· Help teams embed AI into the SDLC in ways that improve productivity, quality, and developer experience.

· Contribute to practical guidance, examples, and playbooks that help teams adopt AI with confidence.

· Partner with stakeholders to ensure adoption is evidence-based and aligned to engineering outcomes.

· Operate with a pragmatic mindset that supports progress while meeting regulatory and risk expectations.


What you will be doing

· Use and evaluate AI tooling used in software engineering, including copilots, assistants, and workflow-integrated capabilities.

· Collaborate directly with engineering teams to improve how AI is used in coding, testing, review, debugging, and documentation.

· Share effective patterns, techniques, and examples that engineers can adopt in their day-to-day work.

· Identify friction points and opportunities for improvement in current engineering workflows.

· Support the rollout of new AI tools, techniques, or approaches into the engineering community.

· Contribute to defining and tracking meaningful success measures such as adoption signals, developer experience indicators, and quality outcomes.

· Work with Security, Model Risk, and other partners to ensure tooling and usage align with ClearBank’s governance expectations.

· Occasionally build or extend lightweight tooling, automation, or workflow enhancements needed to support AI adoption.


What we are looking for

· Strong background in software engineering, platform engineering, DevEx, or DevOps.

· Hands-on experience using AI-assisted development tools in real engineering environments.

· Experience collaborating with delivery teams to improve practices and outcomes.

· Ability to assess tools and approaches based on evidence, practicality, and impact.

· Strong communication skills, able to explain technical concepts clearly to engineers and stakeholders.

· Outcome-driven, curious, and pragmatic in approach.

· Comfortable working across ambiguity and change.

· Collab

source_jobs.title: Senior Technology Governance Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Trust unlocks our greatest potential.

For decades, the clearing of payments remained unchanged and unchallenged. We asked, what if there was a better way? What if we could make banking infrastructure faster, safer, more reliable, and easier to access for all? Here is where you can think radically and responsibly to find the best solution.

What happens when you know you’re inherently trusted? Trust creates safety. Safety encourages you to be brave, to challenge, to fail, to try new things and to execute. At ClearBank everything revolves around trust. We’re not expected to earn it, but we are expected not to break it. We’re trusted to own it. To solve issues in the moment. To know a good risk when we see one, and the judgement to know that ‘can’ doesn’t always mean you ‘should’.



What you can expect:

ClearBank is a place where you can do things differently and challenge the status quo. We will give you a high level of autonomy and transparency, and we trust you to make the right decisions for the bank.

If that excites you, how about joining our IT & Operations team as a Senior Technology Governance Specialist? Reporting to the Head of Technology, you will play a key role in monitoring technology services and maintaining governance, ensuring compliance with outsourcing and regulatory obligations while providing accurate reporting for audits, regulators and senior management.



Your role includes:



Technology Oversight Support:

 - Monitor the delivery of technology services to ensure compliance with outsourcing obligations, promptly escalating deviations, and maintain accurate documentation and oversight evidence to meet regulatory requirements.

Data and Information Security Governance:

 - Support development and oversight of Data and Information Security Governance to meet regulatory and internal standards.

Overall Governance & Coordination:

 - Coordinate with internal teams on technology incidents and remediation, providing timely updates to stakeholders.

 - Maintain and report evidence of compliance with technology-related regulations such as DORA, MiCAR, and cybersecurity obligations.

 - Support technology risk assessments and contribute inputs for risk reporting.

 - Support oversight of ICT third-party supply management activities to ensure compliance with contractual and regulatory requirements.

 - Analyse data from oversight activities to identify trends and provide. actionable insights for governance and strategic decision-making.

 - Prepare governance reports and maintain oversight logs to meet audit and regulatory requirements.

 - Assist in closing management actions from second and third line reviews.

 - Liaise with internal stakeholders to ensure ClearBank Europe requirements are captured and integrated into new initiatives.



What you’ll bring

To excel in this role, you’ll also need:

 - Strong knowledge of technology in financial services and fintech.

 - Strong knowledge of Information Security and Data Governance and Management.

 - Good knowledge of technology related regulations such as DORA, MiCAR and Cybersecurity obligations; practical experience would be a plus.

 - Understanding of crypto asset technologies and their regulatory implications would be a plus.

 - Understanding of Payment Rails (such as SEPA and Target 2) would be a plus

 - Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to interpret data, present actionable insights, and collaborate effectively across teams and geographies.

 - Proactive and detail-oriented, capable of managing multiple priorities with excellent organizational and stakeholder management skills.



What makes a great ClearBank team member? Does this sound like you?

 - You focus on solutions and positive outcomes, thinking radically and responsibly to find the best result for our customers, your colleagues and the bank;

 - You actively seek feedback from others to drive your growth and development, always assuming positive intent from other

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive - FinTech
source_jobs.description:
Senior Account Executive - FinTech

We do things differently around here.

We built ClearBank on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it would be a catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. It would adapt to different needs so a new era in financial services could begin. We’re here to challenge the status quo in banking, are you?

We all know that transforming banking forever is not an easy mission, but we’re more than ready to rise to that challenge. We all do it every day; navigating new, unexpected and sometimes even daunting challenges. But we believe in ourselves and trust we can do it.

Want to innovate? Go for it. Push for a better way? You Can. If you think you have it in you, this is Where You Can.

What you can expect:

ClearBank is a place where you can do things differently and challenge the status quo. We will give you a high level of autonomy and transparency, and we trust you to make the right decisions for the bank.

If that excites you, how about joining our FinTech team, a business within the Client Management department as a Senior Account Executive, reporting to the Head of FinTech. The team is challenging FinTech companies to think differently about how they currently process payments, manage liquidity and reconcile across currencies and jurisdictions.

Your role includes:

 - Offering unique perspectives on how ClearBank’s technology and infrastructure can enhance FinTech companies’ cash management practices.

 - Engaging with client stakeholders across numerous roles and degrees of seniority throughout the sales process.

 - Building and managing a large pipeline of deals, ensuring continuous progress whilst simultaneously maintaining accurate deal reporting & metrics.

 - Taking responsibility for performance against objectives and accountability for delivering on them.

 - Building strong relationships and working collaboratively with Finance, Product, Marketing, Legal and Onboarding teams (among others).

 - Working closely with the Marketing and Communications team to deliver appropriate market-facing presence and messaging.

 - Helping develop the team through sharing your experience, coaching and support.

What you’ll need to excel in this role:

 - Deep interest in FinTech: Cash Management, Banking, Payments and being on the forefront of a rapidly evolving industry and company.

 - Proven experience in Cash Management Sales and/or Relationship Management within the FinTech client segment.

 - Leadership experience, mentoring, coaching and supporting sales teams.

 - Robust knowledge of digital banking solutions such as API’s, along with regulatory and scheme advancements.

 - Excellent communication skills and be able to demonstrate effective listening, questioning and presentation skills, with the ability to tailor information to your audience and take complex topics and simplify them so they are easy to understand.

 - A strong network with and be able to quickly build relationships with stakeholders at all levels within an organisation and to be at ease meeting senior C-level executives.

 - Demonstrable subject matter expertise as it relates to UK FinTechs, and/or payments, SEPA, Currency payments, and Foreign Exchange.

 - Highly self-motivated and driven, willing to challenge yourself and step outside your comfort zone. We need people with high energy and enthusiasm to help us disrupt the FinTech landscape.

 - Resilient and able to overcome the rejection, setbacks and long sales-cycles that can happen in a sales role.

 - Someone who operates with high standards of ethics and integrity.

 - Adaptable, able to think on your feet and problem solve in real-time as needed.



What makes a great ClearBank team member? Does this sound like you?

 - You focus on solutions and positive outcomes, thinking radically and responsibly to find the best result for our customers, your colleagues and the bank;

 - You actively seek feedback from others t

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager - Account Foundations
source_jobs.description:
About us

ClearBank was built on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it’s the catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. That’s why our clients — financial institutions from fintech’s and crypto platforms, to banks and credit unions use our API to power their banking infrastructure.

But we wouldn’t be ClearBank without our people. They’re what powers our innovative technology and the reason we love what we do every day. We’re a group of spirited people who are never afraid to challenge the norm, becoming stronger, more energised and that much better when we’re together. It’s our belief in fairness, autonomy and choice that means our people are empowered with the tools to learn, grow, and contribute to ours and our clients’ success. Interested in joining us? Read on or visit our website for more information.



About you

You’ll be joining as an Engineering Manager reporting to the Director of Engineering. You’ll be a part of a fast-growing business that is challenging the market and doing things differently.

As an Engineering Manager at ClearBank, you will focus on people, delivery, and operational management while occasionally writing and reviewing code. You will lead a team of engineers with a range of skill and experience. Your responsibilities will include driving hiring and interviewing, mentoring and guiding the career development of your direct reports. Reporting to the Director of Engineering and working closely with product partners to prioritise and scope work.

And you’ll be joining one of the fastest-growing tech companies in the UK, according to Deloitte Fast 50.



You will be

 - Leading and managing engineering teams responsible for multiple projects

 - Driving hiring and interviewing processes

 - Mentoring and guiding the career development of direct reports by seeking challenging, impactful work

 - Collaborating with product partners to prioritize and scope work

 - Ensuring quality and testing standards are met

 - Improving the team's security posture

 - Foster a culture of clear, concise communication and active listening

 - Facilitating daily Scrum meetings (daily standups), sprint planning meetings, and retrospective reviews keeping the team on track and managing obstacles that arise

 - Managing 24/7 support when necessary and rapid reaction to issues
   
   

What we are looking for

 - Someone who communicates team strategy and generates enthusiasm and support for strategic goals

 - Strong technical skills in C#, Azure, and SQL. Experience with Azure Data Lake, Data Ingestion and Databricks are highly desirable.

 - Proven track record of leading and managing engineering teams

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills

 - Strong understanding of business value and customer benefit

 - Ability to articulate the customer benefit and understand the underlying problems the team is trying to solve

 - Demonstrate ownership and accountability for team performance

 - Manage ambiguity and uncertainty within the team

 - Promote a culture of documentation and knowledge sharing

 - Facilitate discussions and guide teams towards conclusions and decisions

 - Advocate for process and policy improvements
   
   

About what we offer

 - At ClearBank we have a flexible working philosophy, empowering our employees to manage their time in a way which allows them to meet both work and personal commitments, in partnership with their line manager

 - Spacious and well-equipped new offices in London

 - 27 days annual leave per year, plus flexible bank holidays

 - Option to work abroad for up to 30 days in a 12-month rolling period

 - Regular company-wide socials

 - Take a couple of days per year to volunteer with your chosen charity to do something bigger than ClearBank

 - Private healthcare and discounted dental cover, with employee healthy living perks and discounts

 - Enhanced family-friendly leave

 - Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us

ClearBank was built on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it’s the catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. That’s why our clients — financial institutions from fintech’s and crypto platforms, to banks and credit unions — use our API to power their banking infrastructure.

But we wouldn’t be ClearBank without our people. They’re what powers our innovative technology and the reason we love what we do every day. We’re a group of spirited people who are never afraid to challenge the norm – becoming stronger, more energised, and that much better when we’re together. It’s our belief in fairness, autonomy and choice that means our people are empowered with the tools to learn, grow, and contribute to ours and our clients’ success. Interested in joining us? Read on or visit our website for more information.

About you

You’ll be joining the Team Serval in the Infrastructure Cluster as a Network Engineer. Reporting to an Engineering Manager, you’ll be a part of a fast-growing business that is challenging the market and doing things differently.

You’ll be a playing a critical role in designing, implementing, and maintaining the core networking of our infrastructure platform. Your excited to work with Infrastructure as Code, Networking, and Azure dev ops as you help empower our teams to rapidly deploy and manage their own infrastructure resources and take control of the software delivery lifecycle. You’ll have a proactive approach to collaboration, understanding that we’re stronger as a team than as individuals, contributing to a culture of self-service and continuous improvement. A major part of this role involves working closely with Technical Product Owners and Engineering Managers to shape the art of the possible as we build out our next generation internal platform.

And you’ll be joining one of the fastest-growing tech companies in the UK, according to Deloitte Fast 50.

You will be

 - Design, implement, and manage cloud network infrastructure, ensuring optimal performance and security.

 - Configure and maintain network devices such as routers, switches, and firewalls.

 - Monitor network performance and troubleshoot issues to ensure minimal downtime.

 - Collaborate with the cloud engineering team to integrate and manage Azure public cloud services.

 - Implement and manage VPNs, load balancers, and other network services in Azure.

 - Ensure network security by implementing best practices and staying up-to-date with the latest security trends.

 - Provide support for private cloud network infrastructure, including hardware and software maintenance. We will provide training on this if you do not have data centre experience.

 - Taking part in the exploration and adoption of new technologies and practices

 - Maintaining an eye for continuous improvement, taking part in retrospectives, planning and stand ups

 - Included on the on-call support rota

What we are looking for

 - Hands on knowledge of the Clearbank platform technology stack: Terraform, Azure DevOps Yaml pipeline and templates.

 - Experience in configuring network services in Azure or any other public cloud provider.

 - Ensure network security by implementing best practices and staying up-to-date with the latest security trends.

 - Proven experience in a Platform environment working with a product first mindset

 - Proactive approach to problem, incident and risk management with excellent communication skills

Desirable skills and experience, nice to have but not essential

 - Cisco CCNA is a massive bonus

 - Private cloud network experience

 - Ansible experience at scale

 - Knowledge of BGP and OSPF

A little bit more about us! 



ClearBank is Where You Can belong: 

At ClearBank we think about Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DE&I) a lot. Not because we think we should. But because we know we should – how else can we build a happy workforce and be successful as a business. We’re committed to

source_jobs.title: VP Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
About us

ClearBank was built on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it's the catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. That's why our clients — financial institutions from fintech's and crypto platforms, to banks and credit unions — use our API to power their banking infrastructure.

But we wouldn't be ClearBank without our people. They're what powers our innovative technology and the reason we love what we do every day. We're a group of spirited people who are never afraid to challenge the norm - becoming stronger, more energised, and that much better when we're together. It's our belief in fairness, autonomy and choice that means our people are empowered with the tools to learn, grow, and contribute to ours and our clients' success. Interested in joining us?

Read on or visit our website for more information.

The Role

As VP of IT Infrastructure & Operations you would take accountability for the service levels for our IT Infrastructure for both internal business operations, engineering, and our customer platform.

Tech – You will manage a cross-functional team of support engineers and infrastructure/platform engineers, the team’s goal is to provide next generation technology & platforms that enable the business to excel leveraging cloud technologies

Organisational – As the VP of IT Infrastructure & Operations you will be accountable for the successful strategic direction and execution of changes and support. You will have the autonomy to build/form your teams, manage how you deal with failure and how you measure and celebrate success.

Culture – Promoting and feeding into the companies’ beliefs is key in this role. You will be an ambassador internal and externally. Promoting people through great mentoring and hiring across the team of mixed experience individuals.

The role requires a passionate person who can manage challenging priorities and remove confusion for their team by leveraging data and business insights. The ability to be customer focused and innovative while bringing our stakeholders on the journey and helping shape the strategic direction of the business.



Responsibilities

• Manage and support business critical software and hardware (Data Centres & EUC)

• Manage strategic supplier relationships

• Make business critical buy vs build decisions.

• Drive effective enterprise architecture working closely with Customer and Internal product groups.

• Effective cost management (Cloud cost management, Third Parties, Development cost)

• System uptime and disaster recovery management

• Drive autonomy and efficiencies within engineering teams by removing cognitive load.

• Ensure ClearBank is always adhering to security best practices

• Improve ClearBank’s Monitoring and Alerting strategy

• Mentor and nurture your team. Effective 1:1 management of your leadership team. Skip level 1:1s to understand and help communications with your wider team.

• Leverage modern technologies where appropriate. ClearBank want to lead the financial industry when it comes to technology. Be a champion of those technology choices across the floor.

• Manage Technology wide initiatives to drive best practices and move the platform forward as a whole.

• Enable collaboration and innovation in the team and with stakeholders. Create an environment that drive positive energy and a mentality to succeed.

• Define metrics and ensure we can effectively measure success.

Experience

• Leading people managers.

• Leading technology teams.

• Demonstratable experience of technology judgements and commercially viable decision

• Full ownership of the software delivery lifecycle.

• Able to take business problems and work independently and/or delegate to solve these.

• Using best practice to improve products/services or processes.

• Experience in cloud computing

• Experiencing managing stakeholders

• Risk Management process

• Delivering software in a regulated/controlled environment

source_jobs.title: Communications Executive (Maternity Cover 12 mths FTC)
source_jobs.description:
Communications Executive (Maternity Cover 12 mths FTC)

About you

ClearBank is growing fast with big ambitions, and this role sits at the heart of how we tell that story.

You'll play a key role in delivering ClearBank's external communications activity across the UK and Europe. Working closely with the Head of External Communications, you'll help drive our PR programme, manage agency relationships, coordinate media activity, and ensure communications projects move forward smoothly and efficiently.

This is a hands-on role with a high degree of ownership. You'll act as a trusted partner within a small communications team, balancing day-to-day delivery with longer-term planning, while working closely with marketing colleagues, senior stakeholders and external agencies.

We're looking for someone who can hit the ground running, bring strong communications fundamentals, and thrive in a fast-paced environment where priorities can shift quickly. While the majority of the role focuses on external communications and PR, you'll also support key internal communications activity across the business.

Reporting to the Head of External Communications, you'll play an important role in helping build, protect and elevate ClearBank's profile during an exciting period of growth across multiple markets.

Your role includes:

External Communications & PR

 - Supporting the delivery of ClearBank's communications and PR programme across the UK and Europe

 - Acting as a day-to-day lead across communications activity, ensuring projects, approvals and deadlines remain on track

 - Working closely with external PR agencies, briefing activity, coordinating outputs and ensuring work aligns to business priorities

 - Drafting and delivering a range of communications materials including press releases, media comments, briefing notes, Q&As, talking points and campaign content

 - Coordinating spokesperson opportunities, including arranging interviews, preparing executive briefing materials and supporting media engagement

 - Proactively identifying opportunities to amplify ClearBank's profile through media coverage, thought leadership and industry activity

 - Monitoring media activity and coverage, keeping a close eye on industry developments, competitor activity and emerging opportunities

 - Managing communications trackers and reporting, ensuring visibility of ongoing activity and results

 - Supporting social media content creation where it relates to PR and external communications activity

Executive & Stakeholder Support

 - Working with senior stakeholders across the business, including members of the Executive Team and C-suite

 - Supporting executive visibility across media opportunities, industry events, conferences and LinkedIn activity

 - Attending industry events and supporting spokespersons before, during and after engagements

 - Building strong relationships across Marketing, Revenue, Product and Leadership teams to ensure consistent and aligned messaging

Internal Communications Support

 - Supporting internal communications initiatives in partnership with the Internal Communications team

 - Helping maintain key communication channels including the intranet and Viva Engage

 - Supporting company-wide communications including town halls, business updates, product launches and strategic announcements

 - Ensuring consistency between internal and external messaging

Broader Coordination & Delivery

 - Coordinating awards submissions, speaker opportunities and industry surveys

 - Supporting communications activity across existing and future international markets, particularly Europe

 - Tracking media coverage and communications performance, reporting against agreed KPIs

 - Ensuring external communications opportunities are shared across the wider marketing function for amplification and reuse

What we are looking for

 - 3-5 years' experience in PR, communications or external affairs, either agency-side or in-house

 - Experience manag

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering - Digital Assets
source_jobs.description:
Role Overview

The Director of Engineering - Digital Assets will provide senior technology leadership for ClearBank’s digital assets capability, owning the technical strategy, delivery oversight, and evolution of this domain as it becomes a material payment rail for the bank.

This role will lead an established but early‑stage team working on a greenfield digital assets capability, currently focused on stablecoin minting and redemption. The Director will partner closely with Product partners to shape the long‑term roadmap, ensure robust technical foundations, and guide ClearBank’s progression in this exciting domain.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership

 - Own the technology strategy for Digital Assets, ensuring alignment with ClearBank’s wider platform and technology strategy.

 - Partner with the Head of Product for Digital Assets to define and evolve a clear, credible roadmap covering near‑term delivery and longer‑term technical direction.

 - Provide domain leadership and technical challenge, supporting informed decisions on build, buy, and partner options as the capability matures.

Delivery & Execution

 - Provide senior oversight of an existing Digital Assets engineering team, ensuring delivery remains on track as the product moves from early testing through to go‑live.

 - Ensure strong engineering fundamentals, including resilience, scalability, security, and operational readiness, appropriate for a regulated bank.

 - Support the transition from initial capability to a broader, high impact portfolio of services in the digital assets ecosystem.

Technology & Architecture

 - Guide architectural decisions for integrating with digital asset networks while maintaining ClearBank standards.

 - Define the technical direction of travel for digital assets, including how the platform could evolve to support additional capabilities.

 - Ensure digital assets integrate effectively with ClearBank’s existing services.

People & Leadership

 - Lead and develop engineers and engineering managers within the Digital Assets domain.

 - Set clear expectations, provide coaching, and build a culture of ownership, learning, and delivery.

 - Contribute to the wider Director community, strengthening domain specialism while maintaining consistency across the technology organisation.

Risk, Regulation & Stakeholder Management

 - Work closely with Risk, Compliance, and Legal teams to ensure digital assets capabilities operate within applicable regulatory frameworks.

 - Communicate clearly with senior stakeholders on progress, risks, and trade‑offs in a complex and evolving domain.

 - Ensure appropriate controls, documentation, and operational processes are in place as the product scales.

What We’re Looking For

Essential Experience

 - Senior technology leadership experience (Director or equivalent) within payments, financial services, or adjacent regulated domains.

 - Demonstrable experience owning and evolving complex platforms or payment rails from early delivery into live operation.

 - Strong domain understanding of digital assets or stablecoin‑based systems, sufficient to lead strategy and challenge technical decisions (deep blockchain specialism is not required).

 - Proven ability to partner effectively with Product leadership to shape and deliver long‑term strategy.

Desirable Experience

 - Experience integrating with third‑party financial or digital asset platforms.

 - Exposure to EU regulatory environments for payments or digital assets.

 - Experience scaling teams and platforms as product maturity and customer adoption increase.

Leadership & Behaviours

 - Pragmatic and outcome‑focused, with sound judgement to balance speed, risk, and long‑term value.

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguity and guiding teams through an evolving domain.

 - Clear communicator who can explain complex concepts to both technical and non‑technical audiences.

 - Role‑model for ClearBank’s expectations around ownership, delivery, and col

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering - Payments
source_jobs.description:
Role Overview

The Director of Engineering – UK Payments will provide senior technology leadership for ClearBank’s UK-focused payments capabilities, owning the technical strategy, delivery oversight, and evolution of this domain, providing a highly utilised and relied upon set of capabilities for clients.

This role will lead an established set of teams as they evolve payments capabilities to scale and meet developing client needs. The Director will collaborate closely with Product partners to shape the long‑term roadmap, ensure robust technical foundations, and guide ClearBank’s progression in this vital domain.

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership

 - Own the technology strategy for UK Payments, ensuring alignment with ClearBank’s wider platform and technology strategy.

 - Partner with the Head of Product for UK to define and evolve a clear, credible roadmap covering near‑term delivery and longer‑term technical direction.

 - Provide domain leadership and technical challenge, supporting informed decisions on build, buy, and partner options as the capability matures.

Delivery & Execution

 - Provide senior oversight of an existing UK Payments engineering teams, ensuring delivery remains on track as the products evolve and new products are introduced.

 - Ensure strong engineering fundamentals, including resilience, scalability, security, and operational readiness, appropriate for a regulated bank.

 - Support the evolution of a highly utilised set of capabilities as they scale to meet future client needs.

Technology & Architecture

 - Guide architectural decisions for payments capabilities while maintaining ClearBank standards.

 - Define the technical direction of travel for payments, including how the platform could evolve to support additional capabilities and ease of further expansion.

 - Ensure payments services integrate effectively with ClearBank’s other services.

People & Leadership

 - Lead and develop engineers and engineering managers within the UK Payments domain.

 - Set clear expectations, provide coaching, and build a culture of ownership, learning, and delivery.

 - Contribute to the wider Director community, strengthening domain specialism while maintaining consistency across the technology organisation.

Risk, Regulation & Stakeholder Management

 - Work closely with Risk, Compliance, and Legal teams to ensure UK Payments capabilities operate within applicable regulatory frameworks.

 - Communicate clearly with senior stakeholders on progress, risks, and trade‑offs in a complex and evolving domain.

 - Ensure appropriate controls, documentation, and operational processes are in place as the product scales.

Shape [data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAARnQU1BAACxjwv8YQUAAAAJcEhZcwAADsMAAA7DAcdvqGQAAAANSURBVBhXY2BgYGAAAAAFAAGKM+MAAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC]

What We’re Looking For

Essential Experience

 - Senior technology leadership experience (Director or equivalent) within payments, financial services, or adjacent regulated domains.

 - Demonstrable experience owning and evolving complex platforms, preferably in a high throughput environment.

 - Strong domain understanding of payments, sufficient to develop strategy and challenge technical decisions (deep blockchain specialism is not required).

 - Proven ability to partner effectively with Product leadership to shape and deliver long‑term strategy.

Desirable Experience

 - Experience integrating with third‑party schemes and platforms.

 - Exposure to regulatory environments for payments.

 - Experience scaling teams and platforms as product maturity and customer adoption increase.

Leadership & Behaviours

 - Pragmatic and outcome‑focused, with sound judgement to balance speed, risk, and long‑term value.

 - Comfo

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
About us

ClearBank was built on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it’s the catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. That’s why our clients — financial institutions from fintech’s and crypto platforms, to banks and credit unions — use our API to power their banking infrastructure.

But we wouldn’t be ClearBank without our people. They’re what powers our innovative technology and the reason we love what we do every day. We’re a group of spirited people who are never afraid to challenge the norm – becoming stronger, more energised, and that much better when we’re together. It’s our belief in fairness, autonomy and choice that means our people are empowered with the tools to learn, grow, and contribute to ours and our clients’ success. Interested in joining us? Read on or visit our website for more information.

About you

You’ll be joining as an Engineering Manager reporting to the Director of Engineering. You’ll be a part of a fast-growing business that is challenging the market and doing things differently.

As an Engineering Manager at ClearBank, you will focus on people, delivery, and operational management while occasionally writing and reviewing code. You will lead a team of engineers with a range of skill and experience. Your responsibilities will include driving hiring and interviewing, mentoring and guiding the career development of your direct reports. Reporting to the Director of Engineering and working closely with product partners to prioritize and scope work.



You will be

 - Leading and managing engineering teams responsible for multiple projects

 - Driving hiring and interviewing processes

 - Mentoring and guiding the career development of direct reports by seeking challenging, impactful work

 - Collaborating with product partners to prioritize and scope work

 - Ensuring quality and testing standards are met

 - Improving the team's security posture

 - Foster a culture of clear, concise communication and active listening

 - Facilitating daily Scrum meetings (daily standups), sprint planning meetings, and retrospective reviews keeping the team on track and managing obstacles that arise

 - Managing 24/7 support when necessary and rapid reaction to issues
   
   

What we are looking for

 - Someone who communicates team strategy and generates enthusiasm and support for strategic goals

 - Strong technical skills in C#, Azure, and SQL

 - Proven track record of leading and managing engineering teams

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills

 - Strong understanding of business value and customer benefit

 - Ability to articulate the customer benefit and understand the underlying problems the team is trying to solve

 - Demonstrate ownership and accountability for team performance

 - Manage ambiguity and uncertainty within the team

 - Promote a culture of documentation and knowledge sharing

 - Facilitate discussions and guide teams towards conclusions and decisions

 - Advocate for process and policy improvements

A little bit more about us! 



ClearBank is Where You Can belong: 

At ClearBank we think about Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DE&I) a lot. Not because we think we should. But because we know we should – how else can we build a happy workforce and be successful as a business. We’re committed to making sure our teams reflect society. And we put energy and effort into making this a place where everyone feels at home, where they’re given the trust and encouragement to do their very best work. Here are some of the things we’re most proud of: 

 - 42% of our senior management roles are held by women 

 - Our enhanced family leave policies

 - Launching our partnership with Code First Girls 

 - Increasing awareness of neurodiversity in the workplace through our partnership with the Inclusive Group 



Our hiring process and what you can expect:

Application: a member of our talent acquisition team will review your application aga

source_jobs.title: ER Manager
source_jobs.description:
Grow with the challenge

At the time of inception, cloud-based systems and API fluency were new. But that didn't scare us. We waved goodbye to legacy systems and embraced modern technology – where our true innovation began and didn't stop.

No hierarchy, no egos. Just a spirit of collaboration that flows right throughout our organisation – it’s woven into our DNA. We genuinely want to know about each other’s learnings and expertise, especially where it can help us achieve bigger things.

At ClearBank, we make things happen. We solve problems together because we know that many heads are always better than one. Valuing different perspectives is what sets our culture apart. And being able to disagree and commit is key to our progress. Above all we’re a team because when we win together, it feels so much better than doing it alone.



What you can expect:

ClearBank is a place where you can do things differently and challenge the status quo. We will give you a high level of autonomy and transparency, and we trust you to make the right decisions for the bank.

You will manage the delivery of ClearBank’s employee relations approach by ensuring processes are fair, pragmatic, and aligned with our culture and values. This role focuses on providing case guidance, reporting, building manager capability, and contributing to a positive work environment through proactive ER practices.

Key Responsibilities

 - Provide advice and support to managers and employees on ER matters, including performance, disciplinary, and grievance cases.

 - Assist in developing and maintaining ER policies, procedures, and toolkits that empower managers.

 - Deliver training sessions and coaching to managers to build confidence in handling ER issues.

 - Monitor ER case trends and provide regular reporting for the People Team.

 - Keep up to date with employment legislation and support implementation of required changes.

 - Use data insights to identify risk areas and recommend improvements to employee experience.

 - Collaborate with HR colleagues to ensure consistency and compliance across all ER activities.

Skills & Experience (must-have)

 - Strong knowledge of UK employment law and its practical application.

 - Experience managing ER cases, including performance and disciplinary matters.

 - Exposure to data-driven ER reporting and analysis.

 - Ability to communicate clearly and influence stakeholders at different levels.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and sound judgement in sensitive situations.

 - Organised and able to manage multiple priorities and projects effectively.

 - High level of integrity and ability to maintain confidentiality.

Nice-to-have

 - Experience in a regulated or high-growth environment.

 - Knowledge of EU employment law and its practical application.

A little bit more about us! 



ClearBank is Where You Can belong: 

At ClearBank we think about Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DE&I) a lot. Not because we think we should. But because we know we should – how else can we build a happy workforce and be successful as a business. We’re committed to making sure our teams reflect society. And we put energy and effort into making this a place where everyone feels at home, where they’re given the trust and encouragement to do their very best work. Here are some of the things we’re most proud of: 

 - 42% of our senior management roles are held by women 

 - Our enhanced family leave policies

 - Launching our partnership with Code First Girls 

 - Increasing awareness of neurodiversity in the workplace through our partnership with the Inclusive Group 



Our hiring process and what you can expect:

Application: a member of our talent acquisition team will review your application against the key requirements for the role and let you know within two weeks whether you’re moving forward to the next stage; 

Screening: You’ll spend 30-45 minutes speaking to one of our recruiters learning more about the role and ClearBank, and we’ll get

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager - Payments (BACS & Chaps)
source_jobs.description:
We built ClearBank on the belief that banking infrastructure would no longer slow down progress. Instead, it would be a catalyst that unlocks the potential to innovate. It would adapt to different needs so a new era in financial services could begin. We’re here to challenge the status quo in banking, are you?

We all know that transforming banking forever is not an easy mission, but we’re more than ready to rise to that challenge. We all do it every day; navigating new, unexpected and sometimes even daunting challenges. But we believe in ourselves and trust we can do it. Want to innovate? Go for it. Push for a better way? You Can. If you think you have it in you, this is Where You Can.

About Us

We’re ClearBank – a technology-enabled bank with a mission to transform banking infrastructure. Our cloud-native platform powers payments and banking services for financial institutions, fintechs and regulated clients.

We operate with a strong focus on trust, collaboration and delivering meaningful outcomes for our clients, creating an environment where teams can innovate and move with pace.

Role Purpose

As an Engineering Manager in our Payments team, you will lead a set of teams responsible for building and operating critical UK payment systems, including BACS, CHAPS and SWIFT.

You will be accountable for delivering reliable, scalable and secure payment capabilities while creating a high-performance environment where engineers can thrive.

This role brings together technical leadership, delivery ownership and people leadership, ensuring both strong outcomes and a collaborative, high-trust way of working.

Key Responsibilities

 - Deliver and continuously improve payment services across UK payment rails (BACS, CHAPS, SWIFT).

 - Ensure systems meet high standards for availability, resilience, security and regulatory compliance.

 - Own delivery outcomes in partnership with Product, Technology, Risk and Operations.

 - Lead, coach and develop a team of engineers, supporting both performance and growth.

 - Establish clear priorities and ensure focus on high-impact outcomes.

 - Drive engineering excellence in system design, testing, deployment and observability.

 - Identify risks and dependencies early, taking action to maintain delivery momentum.

 - Foster strong collaboration across teams, ensuring alignment and effective communication.

 - Own operational performance, including incident management and continuous improvement.

 - Promote a strong ownership mindset across the team for production systems.

What success looks like in the role

 - Payment systems are highly reliable, resilient and meet regulatory and operational expectations.

 - The team consistently delivers high-quality outcomes aligned to business priorities.

 - Engineers are engaged, growing and performing at a high level.

 - Cross-functional collaboration is strong, with clear communication and alignment.

 - Continuous improvements are made to system performance, scalability and delivery practices.

Skills & Experience (must-have)

 - Experience leading engineering teams in an ambitious technology environment.

 - Strong understanding of distributed systems, APIs and cloud-native architectures.

 - Knowledge of DevOps practices and CI/CD.

 - Proven track record delivering and operating high-availability production systems.

 - Ability to balance technical depth with delivery focus and pragmatism.

 - Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.

 - Experience working in regulated environments or complex operational settings.

 - Ability to build and maintain high-performing, inclusive and collaborative teams.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ownership mindset.

 - Good awareness of agile delivery practices and a pragmatic approach to implementation.

Nice-to-have

 - Experience with UK payment schemes (such as BACS, CHAPS and SWIFT).

 - Experience in banking, fintech or payments environments.

 - Familiarity with Azure and modern cloud pl

notes: ClearBank is a fintech company with a strong focus on engineering and technology. The job postings suggest a high demand for technical talent, particularly in areas such as AI, platform engineering, and payments.
